meta.title: Welldot — Open well profile format
meta.description: Free editor to create, visualize, and export geological and construction profiles. A simple JSON, versioned, readable by any language.

hero.body: Free editor to create, visualize, and export geological and construction profiles. A simple JSON, versioned, readable by any language.

format.intro: Today, well data lives in PDFs that don't talk to each other. The {well} file is a simple, versioned format to change that — a single JSON that fully describes a well, designed as an {standard}.

props.title: Visualization
props.body: Free D3.js library to render and export geological and construction profiles at real scale.

props.title: Record
props.body: Predictable structure to archive and version well data, ready for submission and auditing.

props.title: Research
props.body: Organized metric fields allow cross-referencing and comparing wells across aquifers, regions, and eras.

props.title: Open
props.body: Human-readable JSON, public specification, and permissive license — free to use, extend, and contribute.

intention.body: Today, a water well's data exists in many places: in the driller's notebook, in the consultant's spreadsheet, in the PDF report submitted to the licensing authority. Each in its own format, each {unreadable}. {welldot} and the {well} format were created to solve this.

pillars.title: Live editor
pillars.body: Tables linked to the drawing. Edit a layer, see the profile redrawn at real scale.

pillars.title: Open format
pillars.body: Human-readable JSON, public specification, and Apache 2.0 license. Implement in any language.

pillars.title: Submission-ready
pillars.body: A4 PDFs at real scale, with header, metadata, and multi-page drawing.

contexts.text1: A single human-readable text file: archivable in folders, versionable in git, indexable in databases, {aiEm}. The core is strict where it matters — {si}, {wgs}, English keys — and {flexible} everywhere else.
contexts.text2: The repository includes a ready-made {skill}: point any agent to {repo} and it operates the format natively.

horizon.intro: v1 covers geological and construction profiles. The specification evolves openly to embrace the full lifecycle of a well.

props.title: Native .las
props.body: Import and storage of LAS geophysical profiles, side by side with the lithological profile.

props.title: Pump tests
props.body: Pump tests, recovery, specific flow, and hydrodynamic parameters as time series.

props.title: Operational history
props.body: Event log: maintenance, pump replacement, static level, shutdowns — dated and attributable.

props.title: Water quality
props.body: Physical-chemical and bacteriological analyses linked to sampling, depth, and date.

props.title: Extended metadata
props.body: Water rights, owner, usage regime, cadastral references, and links to regulatory databases.

props.title: Community extensions
props.body: Namespaces for fields specific to agencies and regions, without breaking the standardized core.

cta.subtitle: Free to use. Free to implement. Open forever.
